description: Evals for 14 professional marketing and sales skills (6 marketing, 8 sales)

evals.prompt: I need to plan a product launch campaign for our new SaaS analytics tool targeting mid-market companies. Budget is $150K for Q3.
evals.expected_output: A complete campaign brief with SMART objectives, audience segmentation, messaging framework, channel strategy matrix, budget allocation, and measurement plan.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name campaign_planning
Includes SMART objectives tied to funnel stages (awareness, consideration, conversion, retention)
Provides budget allocation following the 70/20/10 framework or similar structured breakdown
Defines audience segments with demographics, psychographics, and behavioral criteria

evals.prompt: Create a brand awareness campaign strategy for expanding into the European market. We have no existing presence there and a $500K annual budget.
evals.expected_output: A strategic campaign brief emphasizing top-of-funnel awareness, localized messaging, channel selection for European markets, and phased budget allocation.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name campaign_planning
Addresses market entry challenges and localisation considerations in the messaging framework
Includes an owned/earned/paid channel strategy matrix with European-relevant channels
Provides a measurement plan with attribution model recommendations

evals.prompt: Plan a quick retention campaign for our existing customers. We're seeing 8% monthly churn and need to bring it down to 5% within 90 days.
evals.expected_output: A retention-focused campaign brief with churn reduction objectives, at-risk customer segmentation, re-engagement messaging, and retention-specific KPIs.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name campaign_planning
Sets SMART goals specifically around reducing churn from 8% to 5% within 90 days
Focuses on bottom-funnel and retention stage tactics rather than awareness

evals.prompt: Design a welcome email sequence for new trial users of our project management tool. We want to convert them to paid within 14 days.
evals.expected_output: A multi-email welcome/onboarding sequence with timing, subject lines, content blocks, CTAs, and conversion triggers for trial-to-paid conversion.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name email_sequence_design
Provides a sequence of at least 4-6 emails with specific timing (day 0, day 1, day 3, etc.)
Includes subject line variants, preview text, and CTA hierarchy for each email
Defines exit conditions and branch logic based on user actions

evals.prompt: Build a re-engagement email sequence for leads that went cold after a demo but never signed. We have about 200 of these from the last quarter.
evals.expected_output: A re-engagement email sequence with personalized hooks, value reminders, social proof, and escalation to sales rep follow-up.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name email_sequence_design
Includes re-engagement specific tactics like new feature announcements, case studies, or limited-time offers
Defines a sunset policy for leads that remain unresponsive after the sequence

evals.prompt: Create a nurture email sequence for leads who downloaded our industry whitepaper. The goal is to move them from awareness to requesting a demo.
evals.expected_output: A content-driven nurture sequence that progressively educates and warms leads toward a demo request, with content recommendations per email.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name email_sequence_design
Designs a progressive content journey from educational to product-focused content
Includes email performance benchmarks (open rate, CTR targets) for each stage

evals.prompt: Generate a monthly marketing performance report for February. I need to present this to the executive team next week.
evals.expected_output: An executive-ready marketing performance report with KPI dashboard, funnel analysis, channel attribution, trend analysis, and recommended actions.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name marketing_performance_report
Includes an executive summary with top-line metrics (pipeline, revenue influenced, CAC, ROAS)
Provides funnel analysis with conversion rates between stages
Includes specific action items and recommendations based on performance data

evals.prompt: I need a deep-dive analysis of our paid advertising performance across Google Ads, LinkedIn, and Meta for the last quarter. Focus on attribution and ROAS by channel.
evals.expected_output: A channel-level performance analysis with multi-touch attribution, ROAS comparison, spend efficiency metrics, and optimization recommendations.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name marketing_performance_report
Breaks down performance by channel (Google Ads, LinkedIn, Meta) with comparable metrics
Discusses attribution methodology and provides multi-touch attribution insights

evals.prompt: Give me a high-level overview of marketing ROI this quarter compared to last quarter. Are we on track to hit our annual pipeline targets?
evals.expected_output: A QoQ comparison report with ROI trends, pipeline contribution analysis, and forecast against annual targets.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name marketing_performance_report
Provides quarter-over-quarter comparison with trend indicators
Includes pipeline target tracking with gap analysis and projected trajectory

evals.prompt: Create a competitive analysis brief comparing us against Salesforce and HubSpot. We need this for a board meeting next Thursday.
evals.expected_output: A competitive brief with SWOT analysis for each competitor, positioning map, messaging differentiation matrix, and executive summary.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name competitive_brief_generation
Includes SWOT analysis for each named competitor (Salesforce and HubSpot)
Provides a positioning map showing relative market positions
Includes messaging differentiation showing how to position against each competitor

evals.prompt: We just learned that our biggest competitor raised $50M and is expanding into our core market. Prepare a strategic competitive response brief.
evals.expected_output: A strategic competitive response brief with threat assessment, defensive positioning strategy, and recommended counter-moves.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name competitive_brief_generation
Assesses the competitive threat level and potential market impact of the funding event
Provides specific defensive and offensive strategic recommendations

evals.prompt: I keep losing deals to Competitor X on pricing. Build me a competitive brief focused on how to win against them in head-to-head evaluations.
evals.expected_output: A sales-oriented competitive brief with win/loss analysis, objection handling scripts, and value-based selling strategies against the specific competitor.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name competitive_brief_generation
Addresses pricing objections with specific talk tracks and value justification
Includes battlecard-style quick reference for sales reps in competitive deals

evals.prompt: Review this blog post draft for brand voice consistency: 'Hey folks! Check out our AMAZING new feature that will totally blow your mind. It's like, the best thing ever for your business workflows.'
evals.expected_output: A brand voice compliance review with scoring against voice dimensions, specific feedback on tone/language issues, and suggested revisions.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name brand_voice_review
Scores the content against brand voice dimensions (tone, formality, vocabulary, personality)
Identifies specific issues with informal language, hyperbole, and inconsistency
Provides rewritten alternatives that align with brand guidelines

evals.prompt: We're updating our website copy across all pages. Can you audit the homepage and pricing page copy against our brand voice guidelines and flag any inconsistencies?
evals.expected_output: A page-by-page brand voice audit with consistency scores, flagged sections, and recommended revisions to align voice across pages.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name brand_voice_review
Evaluates cross-page consistency in tone, vocabulary, and messaging
Provides a rubric-based scoring for each page section reviewed

evals.prompt: Run a comprehensive SEO audit on our website. We've noticed organic traffic dropped 30% over the last two months and need to understand why.
evals.expected_output: A full SEO audit covering technical, on-page, content, and off-page factors with a prioritized action plan to recover lost organic traffic.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name seo_audit_comprehensive
Covers all four audit areas: technical SEO, on-page SEO, content quality, and off-page/backlink analysis
Provides a prioritized action plan with impact and effort ratings
Identifies likely causes for the 30% traffic drop based on common SEO issues

evals.prompt: Audit our blog content for SEO optimization. We publish 8 posts per month but barely get any organic search traffic from them.
evals.expected_output: A content-focused SEO audit analyzing keyword targeting, content quality signals, internal linking, and content gap analysis.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name seo_audit_comprehensive
Evaluates content quality factors including keyword targeting, search intent alignment, and E-E-A-T signals
Identifies content gaps and opportunities based on keyword and topic analysis

evals.prompt: Analyze our SEO performance data. Pull together Core Web Vitals, crawl stats, indexation rates, and keyword ranking distribution into a technical SEO health report.
evals.expected_output: A data-driven technical SEO health report with Core Web Vitals analysis, crawl budget optimization, indexation issues, and ranking distribution charts.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name seo_audit_comprehensive
Addresses Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID/INP, CLS) with specific metric targets
Includes crawl efficiency analysis and indexation coverage metrics

evals.prompt: Research Acme Corp before my meeting with their VP of Engineering next Tuesday. They're a Series C fintech company in San Francisco.
evals.expected_output: A comprehensive account research brief covering company overview, recent news/trigger events, stakeholder mapping, technology stack, and potential pain points.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name account_research
Provides company overview including funding stage, size, industry, and key metrics
Identifies trigger events and recent news that create sales opportunities
Includes stakeholder mapping with the VP of Engineering and likely decision-making structure

evals.prompt: Build a target account profile for our ABM campaign. The account is TechGlobal Inc, a Fortune 500 enterprise software company. We need to understand their marketing tech stack and buying committee.
evals.expected_output: An ABM-ready account profile with buying committee map, technology stack analysis, content engagement history, and recommended engagement strategy.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name account_research
Maps the buying committee with roles, influence levels, and likely priorities
Provides a recommended ABM engagement strategy tailored to the account profile

evals.prompt: I need a quick competitive scan on a prospect. They're currently using Competitor Y's platform and I need to understand their likely pain points with that product before my discovery call.
evals.expected_output: A targeted research brief focused on the prospect's current vendor, known pain points with that vendor, and switching triggers to leverage in the discovery call.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name account_research
Identifies common pain points and limitations of the competitor's product
Suggests discovery questions designed to surface dissatisfaction with the current vendor

evals.prompt: Draft a cold outreach email to the CTO of a mid-size logistics company. Our product helps reduce shipping costs by 20% through AI route optimization.
evals.expected_output: A personalized cold outreach email using a proven framework (AIDA, PAS, or BAB) with compelling hook, value proposition, social proof, and clear CTA.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name outreach_drafting
Uses a structured outreach framework (AIDA, PAS, or BAB) with clear sections
Personalizes the message to the CTO role and logistics industry context
Includes a specific, low-friction CTA appropriate for cold outreach

evals.prompt: Create a multi-touch outreach cadence for our SDR team targeting VP-level marketing executives at e-commerce companies. Include email, LinkedIn, and phone touchpoints.
evals.expected_output: A complete multi-channel outreach cadence with 8-12 touchpoints across email, LinkedIn, and phone with specific messaging for each step.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name outreach_drafting
Provides a sequenced cadence with specific timing between touchpoints (e.g., Day 1, Day 3, Day 7)
Includes messaging templates for each channel (email, LinkedIn, phone script)
Varies the messaging angle across touchpoints to avoid repetition

evals.prompt: Write a follow-up email for a prospect who attended our webinar but didn't book a demo. Make it reference specific content from the webinar about data security.
evals.expected_output: A warm follow-up email that references webinar content, provides additional value, and includes a contextual CTA to continue the conversation.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name outreach_drafting
References the specific webinar topic (data security) as a personalization anchor
Provides additional value beyond just asking for a meeting

evals.prompt: Prepare me for a discovery call with the Head of Operations at a manufacturing company. They submitted a demo request mentioning supply chain visibility challenges.
evals.expected_output: A call preparation brief with pre-call research checklist, agenda template, discovery questions, potential objections, and next step recommendations.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name call_preparation
Includes targeted discovery questions about supply chain visibility pain points
Provides an agenda template with time allocations for each section
Lists likely objections with prepared responses

evals.prompt: I have a closing call tomorrow with a 6-figure deal. The prospect has been evaluating us for 3 months and has two remaining concerns: implementation timeline and data migration. Help me prepare.
evals.expected_output: A closing call preparation brief focused on addressing the two specific objections, with negotiation tactics, implementation timeline framework, and closing techniques.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name call_preparation
Addresses both specific concerns (implementation timeline and data migration) with detailed talking points
Includes closing techniques appropriate for a late-stage enterprise deal
Provides a mutual action plan template for post-close next steps

evals.prompt: Prep me for a QBR with our biggest customer. They spend $200K/year with us and I want to expand into two new departments.
evals.expected_output: A QBR preparation brief with account health review, expansion opportunity framework, ROI presentation structure, and upsell discovery questions.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name call_preparation
Structures the QBR agenda around demonstrating ROI and uncovering expansion opportunities
Includes questions designed to uncover needs in the two target departments

evals.prompt: Process this call summary: 45-minute discovery call with Jane Smith, VP Product at DataFlow Inc. She mentioned their current tool can't handle real-time data streams, they need SOC2 compliance, budget is around $50K, and they want to go live by Q4. Decision maker is the CTO who wasn't on the call.
evals.expected_output: A structured call summary with extracted BANT/MEDDIC fields, action items, follow-up email draft, CRM update recommendations, and risk flags.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name call_summary_processing
Extracts key qualification data: budget ($50K), authority (CTO is decision maker), need (real-time data, SOC2), timeline (Q4)
Flags that the decision maker (CTO) was not on the call as a risk item
Generates specific follow-up action items including getting the CTO involved

evals.prompt: Summarize the key outcomes from today's three sales calls and identify any deals that need executive intervention or escalation.
evals.expected_output: An executive summary of call outcomes with deal status updates, escalation recommendations, and strategic guidance for at-risk opportunities.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name call_summary_processing
Provides a concise executive-level summary rather than detailed call notes
Identifies escalation triggers and recommends specific executive actions

evals.prompt: I just finished a tough negotiation call. The prospect wants a 30% discount, asked about annual vs monthly pricing, and threatened to go with a competitor. Process this and tell me what to do next.
evals.expected_output: A call summary with negotiation analysis, competitive threat assessment, recommended pricing strategy response, and next-step action plan.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name call_summary_processing
Categorizes the discount request and competitive threat as negotiation tactics to be addressed
Recommends a pricing response strategy that preserves value

evals.prompt: Review my current sales pipeline. I have 45 deals worth $2.3M in total. I need to know which deals to prioritize this week and which are at risk of slipping.
evals.expected_output: A pipeline health analysis with deal prioritization, risk categorization, velocity metrics, and specific actions for at-risk deals.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name pipeline_review
Categorizes deals by risk level (healthy, at-risk, stalled) with specific criteria
Provides a prioritized action list for the week based on deal stage and close date
Calculates pipeline coverage ratio and identifies if there are enough deals to hit quota

evals.prompt: Give me a pipeline health check across the entire sales team. We need $5M in closed-won this quarter and I want to know if we'll hit it.
evals.expected_output: An executive pipeline review with team-level metrics, quota attainment projections, pipeline coverage analysis, and risk-adjusted forecast.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name pipeline_review
Provides pipeline coverage ratio against the $5M quarterly target
Includes pipeline hygiene metrics (age, stage distribution, conversion rates)
Delivers a risk-adjusted forecast showing likely, best-case, and worst-case scenarios

evals.prompt: My pipeline has a lot of deals stuck in the proposal stage. Can you analyze why and suggest how to move them forward?
evals.expected_output: A stage-specific pipeline analysis identifying bottlenecks at the proposal stage with root cause analysis and deal acceleration tactics.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name pipeline_review
Identifies common reasons deals stall at the proposal stage
Provides specific tactics to accelerate deals through the proposal stage

evals.prompt: Build a sales forecast for Q4. I have $3.2M in pipeline with deals at various stages. I need to present a reliable number to leadership.
evals.expected_output: A weighted sales forecast with deal-level categorization (commit, best-case, upside), scenario analysis, risk adjustments, and confidence intervals.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name sales_forecasting
Applies weighted probability to each deal based on stage and qualification criteria
Provides three scenarios: conservative/commit, likely/best-case, and optimistic/upside
Includes risk adjustments for deal slippage, seasonal factors, and historical accuracy

evals.prompt: I need a revenue forecast that combines our sales pipeline data with historical trends for the annual financial plan. Include seasonality adjustments and compare against last year.
evals.expected_output: A financial-grade revenue forecast with historical trending, seasonality adjustments, year-over-year comparison, and variance analysis.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name sales_forecasting
Incorporates historical win rates and seasonal patterns into the forecast model
Provides year-over-year comparison with growth rate analysis

evals.prompt: We're behind on our annual target by 15%. Forecast whether we can make it up in the remaining two quarters and what it would take.
evals.expected_output: A gap analysis forecast showing the deficit, required run rate, scenario modeling for recovery, and strategic recommendations to close the gap.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name sales_forecasting
Quantifies the gap and calculates the required run rate for the remaining quarters
Models multiple scenarios for closing the gap (increase pipeline, improve win rate, increase deal size)
Provides executive-level recommendations with resource and investment implications

evals.prompt: Create a battlecard for competing against Zendesk. Our sales team loses about 30% of deals where they're a competitor and we need better ammunition.
evals.expected_output: An interactive battlecard with product comparison matrix, sales plays for different scenarios, objection handling scripts, and win/loss insights.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name competitive_intelligence_battlecard
Provides a feature-by-feature comparison matrix between our product and Zendesk
Includes specific objection handling scripts for common Zendesk advantages
Contains sales plays organized by deal scenario (e.g., replacing Zendesk vs. new purchase)

evals.prompt: Build competitive battlecards for our top 3 competitors that we can distribute to the entire go-to-market team. Include positioning guidance for each.
evals.expected_output: A set of three battlecards with consistent format covering positioning, key differentiators, common objections, and competitive talking points for each competitor.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name competitive_intelligence_battlecard
Creates battlecards with a consistent, repeatable format across all three competitors
Includes win/loss analysis insights and patterns for each competitor

evals.prompt: I'm going into a bake-off against Monday.com tomorrow. Give me a quick competitive battlecard focused on enterprise features and security where we have an edge.
evals.expected_output: A focused battlecard emphasizing enterprise and security differentiators against Monday.com with quick-reference talking points and trap-setting questions.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name competitive_intelligence_battlecard
Focuses specifically on enterprise features and security as the primary comparison dimensions
Includes trap-setting discovery questions that highlight competitor weaknesses

evals.prompt: Create a one-pager for our enterprise plan targeting healthcare companies. It should highlight HIPAA compliance, integration capabilities, and our customer success program.
evals.expected_output: A structured one-pager with compelling headline, key value propositions, feature highlights, social proof, and call to action tailored to healthcare enterprises.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name sales_asset_creation
Structures the one-pager with clear sections (headline, value props, features, proof, CTA)
Highlights all three requested topics: HIPAA compliance, integrations, and customer success
Includes healthcare-specific language and compliance-focused messaging

evals.prompt: Write a customer case study based on our work with RetailMax. They saw 40% cost reduction and 3x faster processing after implementing our solution 6 months ago.
evals.expected_output: A structured case study with situation/challenge, solution, results, and customer quotes following the standard case study format.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name sales_asset_creation
Follows the challenge/solution/results case study structure
Prominently features the quantitative results (40% cost reduction, 3x faster processing)
Includes placeholder for customer quotes and visual result callouts

evals.prompt: Build an ROI calculator template I can customize for each prospect. It should show cost savings, productivity gains, and payback period for our platform.
evals.expected_output: An ROI calculator framework with input variables, calculation methodology, output metrics (NPV, payback period, total savings), and presentation template.

assertions.text: Uses use_skill with skill_name sales_asset_creation
Defines clear input variables that can be customized per prospect
Calculates key ROI metrics including payback period, total cost savings, and productivity gains
Provides a presentation-ready format with visual layout recommendations
